channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Africa Hockey Teams Gear Up for World Cup with Home Test Series
item.description: South Africa's men's and women's hockey teams are intensifying their preparations for the FIH Hockey World Cup through a series of high-stakes home Test matches. The men's team, competing against Mala...

item.title: JD Vance Reflects on How Life as Vice President Could Foster a Sense of Entitlement
item.description: Vice President JD Vance acknowledged during an appearance on Mike Rowe's podcast, 'The Way I Heard It,' that the privileges of serving in high office could lead him to develop an unhealthy sense of en...

item.title: Uganda and Tanzania Strengthen Trade and Investment Ties
item.description: Uganda has intensified efforts to strengthen trade and investment ties with Tanzania, as businesses from the neighboring country seek new commercial opportunities in one of East Africa's fastest-growi...

item.title: PM Shehbaz urges restraint to preserve regional peace amid US-Iran tensions
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has called on Iran and all other parties involved in the Middle East conflict to exercise restraint and avoid actions that could undermine recent peace achievements. The ...

item.title: ED Files Chargesheet Against Kolkata Officer in Bengal Land Grabbing Case
item.description: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has filed a PMLA chargesheet against suspended Kolkata police deputy commissioner Shantanu Sinha Biswas in a multi-crore property-grabbing and illegal land deals case....

item.title: Man Arrested for ₦3 Billion Cyber Fraud Targeting Financial Institution
item.description: The Police Special Fraud Unit (PSFU) in Lagos has arrested Adeleke Hafees, a suspected member of a cybercrime syndicate, over an alleged ₦3 billion fraud targeting a financial institution. The suspect...

item.title: France advances to semi-finals with Mbappé's crucial goal against Morocco
item.description: France secured their place in the World Cup semi-finals with a 2-0 victory over Morocco, thanks to goals from Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé. Mbappé's eighth goal of the tournament extended his lea...

item.title: Saudi Arabia Supports Somali Military Training Program
item.description: A Saudi military delegation visited two training camps in Galgadud Region, Somalia, to inspect a program funded by Saudi Arabia. The initiative aims to train 5,107 Somali soldiers, including 2,000 rec...

item.title: Essential Guidelines for Women Preparing for Cesarean Delivery
item.description: Cesarean sections, commonly known as C-sections, have become a common method of childbirth worldwide. While this procedure can be life-saving for both mother and child in certain circumstances, it req...

item.title: Cebu City Enforces Truck Ban to Reduce Traffic Congestion
item.description: Cebu City has implemented a temporary truck ban to manage traffic congestion around the venue of BINI’s 'Signals' concert. The decision comes as authorities anticipate high foot traffic and vehicle in...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Draws on July 10, 2026, Have No Winners
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that there were no winners for the major lotto jackpot prizes offered on Friday, July 10, 2026. Both the Ultra Lotto 6/58 and Megalotto 6/45 ...

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Analyzing the Final Eight Teams' Chances to Win
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 has progressed to the quarterfinal stage, with eight teams vying for the championship. The article evaluates each contender's strengths and challenges. Switzerland, making thei...

item.title: Structural issues delay Pfizer office-to-housing conversion in New York
item.description: A structural failure at the former Pfizer headquarters in Midtown Manhattan has raised concerns about the challenges of converting office buildings into residential housing. Steel columns buckled on t...

item.title: Kenyan Woman Honored as Elite US Army Recruiter
item.description: Eve Gatah, a Kenyan-born soldier serving in the United States Army, has been recognized as a top recruiter within the force. Her achievement is attributed to divine favor and hard work, inspiring Keny...

item.title: Kasarani Family Struggles to Bury Relative Due to Unpaid Medical Bills
item.description: Joseph Njoroge died on 28 May at Kenyatta University Referral Hospital after being admitted in March following a stroke, leaving behind a KSh 1.7 million medical bill. His family has been unable to gi...

item.title: Balochistan Chief Minister Announces 75 Terrorists Killed in Recent Operations
item.description: Balochistan Chief Minister Sarfraz Bugti announced that 75 terrorists have been killed in intelligence-based operations across the province since July 5. He highlighted the success of Operation Shaban...

item.title: Wildfire forces evacuation near small B.C. highway community as residents monitor blaze
item.description: A wildfire has erupted near a small highway community in Vancouver's East End, prompting local residents to observe the rapidly spreading flames. The incident, which occurred on July 10, 2026, has rai...

item.title: Joburg Music and Comedy Events This Weekend
item.description: This weekend, Johannesburg will host a variety of entertainment events featuring top local artists. Thandiswa Mazwai will perform at The Soirée: Courtside Couture event in Melrose Park on Saturday, Ju...

item.title: Tinubu unveils infrastructure plan to revamp Wasa as economic hub
item.description: President Bola Tinubu announced plans to upgrade Wasa District into a modern commercial hub in Abuja's Federal Capital Territory (FCT) by providing premium infrastructure. During an inauguration event...

item.title: Public prefers Count Binface over Nigel Farage in Clacton by-election poll
item.description: A new poll reveals that 33% of British adults would prefer Count Binface to win the Clacton by-election, compared to 21% for Nigel Farage. The survey highlights growing public scrutiny of Farage’s fin...
